strengths: 15+ years of full-stack experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, well beyond the 6-year requirement
Current role includes TypeScript/Node.js product work and AI-agent infrastructure (MCP code generators, agentic tooling)
Strong engineering-craft signals: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing workshops, and open-source test libraries
concerns: No evidence of Nest.js, Vitest/Jest/Playwright, Kotlin, or Python — most of the required stack is unproven; React appears in only one role
Career depth is concentrated in niche technologies (DLang, Ember.js); the flagship projects and open-source portfolio center on D rather than the hiring stack
Pattern of short tenures (several roles under 2 years) plus an ongoing founder commitment since 2016 raises retention and focus risk

key_factors.factor: Missing core stack coverage (Nest.js, Vitest, Kotlin, Python)
Experience concentrated in niche tech (DLang, Ember.js) over required frameworks
Job-hopping tenure pattern and competing side venture

response.justification: While the candidate is an experienced generalist with genuine craftsmanship credentials, the fit for this specific role is weak. The position demands proficiency across TypeScript, Nest.js, React, Vitest, Kotlin/Java, and Python, yet the resume shows no Nest.js, no named modern JS testing framework, no Kotlin, no Python, and only a single role using React — with much of the candidate's demonstrated depth invested in DLang and Ember.js, technologies irrelevant to this stack. AI-agent exposure is real but recent (roughly one year). Combined with a history of sub-two-year tenures and a long-running founder side project competing for attention, the candidate would arrive needing to learn most of the primary stack while presenting elevated retention risk for a mid-senior IC seat.
